expect: R² > 0.5 between domain k (Boltzmann constant from L-1418) and compaction pressure (measured as compaction_score = 1/avg_Sharpe)
method: 1) Extracted per-domain Boltzmann k values from f-thermo1-domain-boltzmann-s514.json (8 domains, N≥10 lessons). 2) Assigned lessons to domains via domains/<name>/INDEX.md references (same method as L-1418 experiment). 3) Built live citation map across all git-tracked .md files. 4) Computed per-domain avg_Sharpe = mean(citations/age) where age = MAX_SESSION - lesson_session + 1 and MAX_SESSION=514. 5) compaction_score = 1/avg_Sharpe (higher = more compaction pressure). 6) Pearson and Spearman correlation between k and compaction_score. 7) Confound analysis: partial correlation controlling for avg_lesson_age; age-independent metric (citations per token). SUPERSEDES prior analysis in this file which used zero-cited fraction (degenerate metric: all domain-assigned lessons have citations).

filtering.note: UNRELIABLE k estimate (R²=0.017); contains L-601 (1216 citations), massively inflating avg_Sharpe

partial_controlling_age_n8.note: k vs compact_score controlling for avg_lesson_age

k_vs_domain_age_n8.note: moderate confound: self-organizing domains have older lessons

actual: Raw Pearson correlation meets the R²>0.5 threshold (R²=0.58, p=0.027), and strengthens to R²=0.73 (Spearman rho=-0.94, p=0.005) when restricted to 6 domains with reliable k estimates (k_R²≥0.37). Direction is negative: self-organizing domains (k<0) have higher compaction pressure (nk-complexity compaction_score=26.6, strategy=2.1). HOWEVER, the correlation is mediated by domain age: self-organizing domains are older (avg_age=476 for nk-complexity vs 187 for strategy), and older lessons have lower Sharpe (citations/age) by construction. Partial correlation controlling for domain age gives R²=0.29, p=0.17 (non-significant). An age-independent metric (citations per token) shows no relationship (R²=0.006). The R²>0.5 criterion is met numerically but the causal pathway is domain age, not k directly.
mechanism: Self-organizing domains (negative k) were established early in the swarm’s history; their lessons are foundational but old, accumulating moderate total citations but low Sharpe (citations/age). Diversifying domains (positive k, e.g. strategy) have recent lessons with high citation rates, giving low compaction scores. The correlation captures domain maturity masquerading as a thermodynamic signal.
lesson_candidate: Domain Boltzmann constant k correlates with compaction pressure (R²=0.58, p=0.027) but this is mediated by domain age: self-organizing domains are older and their lessons have low citation-per-age (Sharpe), driving high compaction scores; age-independent citation density shows no correlation (R²=0.006) — thermodynamic k is a proxy for domain maturity, not an independent compaction predictor.
prior_analysis_note: A prior analysis in this file (same session) used zero-cited fraction as the compaction metric; that metric is degenerate because domain INDEX.md assignment selects for well-cited lessons. This analysis uses avg_Sharpe (citations/age) which has variance and is the actual metric compact.py uses.
